title: Olga Tellis v. Bombay Municipal Corporation
legal_principles: Right to livelihood as part of Right to Life
summary: Pavement dwellers challenged eviction from Bombay's footpaths. The Court held that the right to livelihood is included in the right to life under Article 21 because no person can live without the means of living (livelihood). The state cannot deprive a person of livelihood without following a just and fair procedure.
key_holdings: Right to livelihood is part of the right to life under Article 21
Eviction without opportunity to be heard violates Article 21
Pavement dwellers cannot be evicted without alternative arrangements
The sweep of the right to life is wide and far-reaching
key_quotes: The right to life includes the right to livelihood.
An easement like right has been acquired over footpaths by pavement dwellers.
why_important: Established right to livelihood as a constitutional right under Article 21. Foundation for numerous public interest litigations on housing and employment rights.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Right to livelihood impacts labour law and employment-related financial decisions.
